question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a type confusion attack by passing a StreamFile<?> implementation whose getType() returns BALANCE but whose actual data structure is a RECORD file, bypassing the type-based routing in BatchStreamFileNotifier and causing the wrong parser to process the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Order(Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE) annotation on BatchStreamFileNotifier to understand the notifier execution order and craft an attack that targets the first notifier in the CompositeStreamFileNotifier chain, knowing that a failure there will prevent all subsequent notifiers from executing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier's buffer ArrayList is not synchronized and is only accessed by a single subscriber thread, but the queue.put() in notify() is called from a different thread, to create a happens-before violation if the JVM reorders operations, leading to stale data being parsed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger an integer overflow in BatchStreamFileNotifier's files.incrementAndGet() by submitting Long.MAX_VALUE files, causing the counter to wrap to Long.MIN_VALUE and permanently preventing the maxFiles flush threshold from being reached?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of input validation in the StreamFileNotifier.verified() interface to pass a StreamFile<?> with a null getName(), causing a NullPointerException in downstream logging or hash chain verification that references the file name?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CompositeStreamFileNotifier holds a List<StreamFileNotifier> that is injected at construction time, and if that list is mutable, inject a malicious StreamFileNotifier implementation that intercepts all verified() calls and exfiltrates StreamFile data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a resource leak by triggering an exception inside BatchStreamFileNotifier's verified() after the StreamFile is enqueued but before it is processed, leaving the file in the queue indefinitely and consuming memory?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier's StreamFileSubscriber.run() catches InterruptedException and returns, permanently terminating the subscriber thread, by sending an interrupt signal to the thread and then submitting files that will never be processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum queue size enforcement at the StreamFileNotifier interface level to bypass the ArrayBlockingQueue capacity limit in BatchStreamFileNotifier by using a custom StreamFileNotifier implementation that accepts unlimited files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFile<?> whose copy() method returns a reference to the same object rather than a deep copy, causing the lastStreamFile cache in Downloader to hold a reference to the live object, which can then be mutated to corrupt hash chain verification for subsequent files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the StreamFileNotifier interface is public and @NullMarked but has no access control, allowing any Spring bean or component that can obtain the notifier reference to call verified() with arbitrary data, bypassing the intended downloader-only invocation path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a log injection attack by crafting a StreamFile<?> whose toString() method returns a string containing newline characters or ANSI escape codes, which is then logged by BatchStreamFileNotifier's log.debug('Published {}', streamFile), corrupting log files or misleading operators?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier's flush() method uses AtomicLong for files and items counters but the buffer ArrayList is not atomic, creating a TOCTOU window where the flush decision is made based on stale counter values, leading to incorrect batch boundaries?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier's reset() method clears the buffer and resets counters but does not hold any lock, allowing a concurrent verified() call to enqueue a file between the buffer.clear() and files.set(0L) calls, causing that file to be lost from the batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the StreamFileNotifier.verified() method has no return value or acknowledgment mechanism, making it impossible for the caller to know if the file was successfully enqueued or processed, allowing silent failures to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier's StreamFileSubscriber uses a single-threaded executor per file type, so a slow or blocking parse operation for one file type does not affect the other, but a crash in one subscriber thread permanently halts processing for that file type without alerting operators?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the StreamFileNotifier interface does not enforce that verified() is called only after cryptographic verification, allowing a compromised or buggy caller to invoke verified() on an unverified StreamFile, injecting unvalidated data into the parse pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a slow-drain DoS by submitting StreamFile objects with getCount() values just below the maxItems threshold, preventing batch flushes from occurring and causing the buffer to grow until the flushInterval triggers, significantly delaying data availability?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier's exceedsInterval() uses Instant.now() which is not monotonic on all JVM implementations, causing the flush interval check to behave incorrectly under clock skew and either never flushing or flushing too frequently?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the StreamFileNotifier interface is implemented by multiple classes (BatchStreamFileNotifier, CompositeStreamFileNotifier, BlockStreamVerifier's internal usage) and the @Primary annotation on CompositeStreamFileNotifier means all verified() calls go through it, so a single malicious notifier in the composite list can intercept all stream file notifications?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier's StreamFileSubscriber.handle() adds the streamFile to the buffer before checking flush conditions, meaning a malicious StreamFile that causes flush() to throw an exception will have already been added to the buffer, potentially being parsed in a future flush with corrupted state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the StreamFileNotifier interface has no versioning or schema validation, allowing a StreamFile<?> with an incompatible internal structure to be passed to verified() and cause type cast exceptions or data corruption in the downstream parser?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier's StreamFileSubscriber.notify() uses queue.put() which blocks indefinitely when the queue is full, causing the thread calling verified() to be blocked for an unbounded duration, effectively creating a denial-of-service on the downloader thread?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the StreamFileNotifier interface does not define any transaction boundary, allowing a partial failure in CompositeStreamFileNotifier's notifier loop to leave some notifiers updated and others not, creating an inconsistent distributed state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier's buffer is an ArrayList that grows without bound until a flush is triggered, by submitting files that never meet any flush condition (e.g., always below maxFiles, maxItems, window, and flushInterval), causing unbounded memory growth?
